content_markdown: Which of the following pressure-volume ($p$-$V$) diagrams best represents a Carnot cycle?

diagram_metadata.description: Four different p-V diagrams showing closed thermodynamic cycles. Option A shows a cycle with a horizontal top and vertical right side. Option B shows a cycle with a horizontal top and horizontal bottom. Option C shows a cycle bounded by four curves (two isotherms and two adiabats). Option D shows a cycle with straight diagonal lines.

vibe_explanation: A Carnot cycle consists of four reversible processes: two isothermal processes and two adiabatic processes. On a p-V diagram, these appear as four distinct curves. Isotherms follow $p \propto 1/V$, while adiabats are steeper, following $p \propto 1/V^\gamma$. Diagram C is the only one showing four curved paths consistent with these processes, whereas the others include isobaric (horizontal) or isochoric (vertical) segments which are not part of a Carnot cycle.
